  2488→
  2489→== Compliance Rules
  2490→
  2491→This section specifies compositional requirements between motifs.
  2492→When a term voices a particular motif, these rules define what companion elements are required.
  2493→
  2494→=== Procedure Documentation Completeness
  2495→
  2496→Voicing as
  2497→{axo_procedure}
  2498→requires documentation sections for:
  2499→
  2500→* {axs_inputs}
  2501→— what the procedure requires to execute
  2502→
  2503→* {axs_behavior}
  2504→— what the procedure does
  2505→
  2506→* {axs_outputs}
  2507→— what the procedure produces
  2508→
  2509→* {axs_completion}
  2510→— how the procedure signals success or failure
  2511→
  2512→Optional sections that MAY be included:
  2513→
  2514→* {axs_preconditions}
  2515→— environmental state requirements before execution
  2516→
  2517→* {axs_postconditions}
  2518→— environmental state guarantees after success
  2519→
  2520→* {axs_errors}
  2521→— failure modes and recovery guidance
  2522→
  2523→=== Command Documentation Completeness
  2524→
  2525→Voicing as
  2526→{axo_command}
  2527→requires all
  2528→{axo_procedure}
  2529→sections plus:
  2530→
  2531→* {axe_environment}
  2532→dimension specifying execution context (e.g.,
  2533→{axe_bash_interactive},
  2534→{axe_bash_unattended})
  2535→
  2536→=== Guide Documentation Completeness
  2537→
  2538→Voicing as
  2539→{axo_guide}
  2540→requires all
  2541→{axo_procedure}
  2542→sections plus:
  2543→
  2544→* {axs_behavior}
  2545→steps use
  2546→{axe_human_guide}
  2547→control voicings
  2548→
  2549→* Steps describe actions the human performs in external systems
  2550→
  2551→=== Lifecycle Dimension Requirement
  2552→
  2553→Every
  2554→{axo_procedure}
  2555→voicing requires exactly one lifecycle dimension:
  2556→
  2557→* {axd_transient}
  2558→— executes and completes (most common)
  2559→
  2560→* {axd_longrunning}
  2561→— runs until stopped
  2562→
  2563→* {axd_periodic}
  2564→— runs on schedule
  2565→
  2566→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  2567→
  2568→=== Long-running Procedure Completeness
  2569→
  2570→Voicing with
  2571→{axd_longrunning}
  2572→requires all
  2573→{axo_procedure}
  2574→sections plus:
  2575→
  2576→* {axs_startup}
  2577→— initialization behavior and readiness criteria
  2578→
  2579→* {axs_shutdown}
  2580→— graceful termination and cleanup
  2581→
  2582→Optional sections:
  2583→
  2584→* {axs_health}
  2585→— liveness and readiness probes
  2586→
  2587→* {axs_signals}
  2588→— Unix signal handling
  2589→
  2590→* {axs_recovery}
  2591→— crash recovery behavior
  2592→
  2593→=== Periodic Procedure Completeness
  2594→
  2595→Voicing with
  2596→{axd_periodic}
  2597→requires all
  2598→{axo_procedure}
  2599→sections plus:
  2600→
  2601→* {axs_schedule}
  2602→— timing specification
  2603→
  2604→Optional sections:
  2605→
  2606→* {axs_idempotency}
  2607→— re-run safety guarantees
  2608→
  2609→* {axs_overlap}
  2610→— concurrent execution handling
  2611→
  2612→=== CLI Subcommand Completeness
  2613→
  2614→Voicing as
  2615→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  2616→requires:
  2617→
  2618→* Exit Status section voiced as
  2619→{axa_exit_uniform}
  2620→or
  2621→{axa_exit_enumerated}
  2622→(which voice
  2623→{axs_completion}
  2624→in CLI context)
  2625→
  2626→* Arguments section voiced as
  2627→{axa_argument_list}
  2628→(which voices
  2629→{axs_inputs}
  2630→in CLI context; may list zero arguments if operation takes none)
  2631→
  2632→=== CLI Option Completeness
  2633→
  2634→Voicing as
  2635→{axa_cli_option}
  2636→requires the definition text to specify:
  2637→
  2638→* Long form (e.g., `--file`)
  2639→
  2640→* Short form or explicit absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  2641→
  2642→* Default value, if the argument is used with
  2643→{axd_defaulted}
  2644→
  2645→=== Argument List Item Qualification
  2646→
  2647→Within an
  2648→{axa_argument_list},
  2649→each item requires a preceding
  2650→{axd_dimension}
  2651→annotation specifying at minimum:
  2652→
  2653→* {axd_required}
  2654→or
  2655→{axd_optional}
  2656→
  2657→* {axd_defaulted}
  2658→if a default value applies
  2659→
  2660→=== Type Voicing Qualification
  2661→
  2662→Voicing a type (subspecializing
  2663→{axt_*}
  2664→or
  2665→{axtu_*}
  2666→motifs) constrains dimension usage.
  2667→
  2668→Valid dimensions:
  2669→
  2670→* {axd_list}
  2671→— type intrinsically represents delimited collection
  2672→
  2673→Invalid dimensions:
  2674→
  2675→* {axd_required},
  2676→{axd_optional},
  2677→{axd_defaulted}
  2678→— presence applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2679→
  2680→* {axd_repeated}
  2681→— repetition applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2682→
  2683→=== Regime Specification Completeness
  2684→
  2685→Voicing as
  2686→{axrg_regime}
  2687→requires:
  2688→
  2689→* Format dimension (
  2690→{axf_bash},
  2691→{axf_makefile},
  2692→{axf_json},
  2693→etc.) specifying how assignments are carried
  2694→
  2695→* Provenance dimension (
  2696→{axrd_file_sourced},
  2697→{axrd_env_inherited},
  2698→or
  2699→{axrd_constructed})
  2700→declaring how
  2701→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2702→arrive
  2703→
  2704→=== Regime Variable Qualification
  2705→
  2706→Voicing as
  2707→{axrg_variable}
  2708→requires:
  2709→
  2710→* Type in annotation (base type motif or type voicing)
  2711→
  2712→* At minimum
  2713→{axd_required}
  2714→or
  2715→{axd_optional}
  2716→
  2717→* {axd_defaulted}
  2718→if a default value applies
  2719→
  2720→Valid dimensions:
  2721→
  2722→* {axd_required}
  2723→— variable must have assignment
  2724→
  2725→* {axd_optional}
  2726→— variable may be absent
  2727→
  2728→* {axd_defaulted}
  2729→— fallback value when absent
  2730→
  2731→* {axd_list}
  2732→— inherited from type voicing; variable's type is a delimited collection
  2733→
  2734→Invalid dimensions:
  2735→
  2736→* {axd_repeated}
  2737→— regime files use unique keys; multiple assignments of same variable not permitted
  2738→
  2739→* {axd_nullable}
  2740→— format-dependent;
  2741→{axf_bash}
  2742→has no null distinct from empty string
  2743→
  2744→=== Regime Voicing Annotation Completeness
  2745→
  2746→{axvr_regime}
  2747→requires:
  2748→
  2749→* {axf_format}
  2750→dimension specifying how
  2751→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2752→are carried
  2753→
  2754→{axvr_variable}
  2755→requires:
  2756→
  2757→* Second
  2758→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2759→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2760→
  2761→* {axd_required}
  2762→or
  2763→{axd_optional}
  2764→
  2765→* Type
  2766→{axl_motif}
  2767→(base type or type voicing)
  2768→
  2769→{axvr_group}
  2770→requires:
  2771→
  2772→* Second
  2773→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2774→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2775→
  2776→=== Regime Hierarchy Consistency
  2777→
  2778→* {axvr_group}
  2779→with
  2780→{axd_conditional}
  2781→requires corresponding
  2782→{axhrgb_group}
  2783→in subdocument to have at least one
  2784→{axhrgc_gate}
  2785→
  2786→* {axvr_group}
  2787→without
  2788→{axd_conditional}:
  2789→corresponding
  2790→{axhrgb_group}
  2791→must have no
  2792→{axhrgc_gate}
  2793→
  2794→* Every
  2795→{axvr_variable}
  2796→under a regime should appear as either
  2797→{axhrv_variable}
  2798→or
  2799→{axhrgv_variable}
  2800→in the subdocument
  2801→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrv_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define container vessel configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrv_regime}
     7→instance represents a vessel image source, either bound from an external registry or conjured from a Dockerfile.
     8→RBRV assignment files are found in `rbev-vessels/*/rbrv.env` and configure image sourcing for bottle deployments.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrv_sigil}
    12→
    13→Unique identifier for the vessel.
    14→Must be 1-64 characters forming a valid cross-context identifier.
    15→Must match the vessel directory name.
    16→
    17→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    18→{rbrv_description}
    19→
    20→Human-readable description of the vessel purpose.
    21→Optional; 0-512 characters.
    22→
    23→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    24→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    25→
    26→Selects vessel provisioning strategy.
    27→Must be
    28→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
    29→or
    30→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
    31→
    32→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    33→{rbrv_group_binding}
    34→
    35→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    36→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    37→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
    38→
    39→Variables in this group apply only when
    40→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    41→is
    42→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}.
    43→Binding retrieves a pre-built image from an external registry without local build steps.
    44→
    45→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    46→{rbrv_bind_image}
    47→
    48→Fully-qualified image reference to copy from an external registry.
    49→Must be a valid FQIN; 1-512 characters.
    50→
    51→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    52→{rbrv_group_conjuring}
    53→
    54→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    55→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    56→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
    57→
    58→Variables in this group apply only when
    59→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    60→is
    61→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
    62→Conjuring builds a vessel image locally from a Dockerfile and build context in the repository.
    63→
    64→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    65→{rbrv_conjure_dockerfile}
    66→
    67→Path to Dockerfile relative to repository root.
    68→Must be 1-512 characters.
    69→
    70→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    71→{rbrv_conjure_bldcontext}
    72→
    73→Build context path relative to repository root.
    74→Must be 1-512 characters.
    75→
    76→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    77→{rbrv_conjure_platforms}
    78→
    79→Space-separated list of target platforms for multi-platform builds.
    80→Must be 1-512 characters.
    81→Examples: `linux/amd64`, `linux/amd64 linux/arm64`.
    82→
    83→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    84→{rbrv_conjure_binfmt_policy}
    85→
    86→Controls whether cross-platform emulation via binfmt is permitted during builds.
    87→Must be
    88→{rbrv_binfmt_allow}
    89→or
    90→{rbrv_binfmt_forbid}.
    91→
    92→// ⟦axhro_broach⟧
    93→{rbkro_broach}
    94→
    95→Sets defaults for all RBRV_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
    96→Detects unexpected RBRV_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
    97→Builds ZRBRV_ROLLUP serialization string.
    98→
    99→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   100→{rbkro_validate}
   101→
   102→Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
   103→Checks RBRV_BIND_IMAGE or RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE presence invariant.
   104→Validates RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY enum constraint.
   105→
   106→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   107→{rbkro_render}
   108→
   109→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
   110→Sections: Core Vessel Identity, Binding Configuration, Conjuring Configuration.
   111→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   112→Validates after full render completes.
   113→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrn_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define bottle service deployment configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrn_regime}
     7→instance represents a distinct service deployment with its own network, entry, and uplink settings.
     8→RBRN assignment files are found in `Tools/rbw/rbrn_*.env` and configure container deployment and network security.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrn_moniker}
    12→
    13→Unique moniker for the bottle service instance.
    14→Must be 2-12 characters and form a valid cross-context identifier.
    15→
    16→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    17→{rbrn_description}
    18→
    19→Human-readable description of the service purpose.
    20→Optional; 0-120 characters.
    21→
    22→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    23→{rbrn_runtime}
    24→
    25→Container runtime for service deployment.
    26→Must be
    27→{rbrn_runtime_docker}
    28→or
    29→{rbrn_runtime_podman}.
    30→
    31→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    32→{rbrn_group_ark_reference}
    33→
    34→Ark references identify which vessel consecration to deploy.
    35→At runtime, these resolve against the repository configuration to form complete image paths.
    36→
    37→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    38→{rbrn_sentry_vessel}
    39→
    40→Vessel identifier for the sentry container image.
    41→
    42→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    43→{rbrn_sentry_consecration}
    44→
    45→Consecration timestamp for the sentry container image.
    46→
    47→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    48→{rbrn_bottle_vessel}
    49→
    50→Vessel identifier for the bottle container image.
    51→
    52→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    53→{rbrn_bottle_consecration}
    54→
    55→Consecration timestamp for the bottle container image.
    56→
    57→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    58→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    59→
    60→Selects entry service behavior for user-initiated connections.
    61→
    62→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    63→{rbrn_group_entry}
    64→
    65→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    66→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    67→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}
    68→
    69→Variables in this group apply only when
    70→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    71→is
    72→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}.
    73→
    74→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    75→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
    76→
    77→External port exposed on the transit network for user entry.
    78→Must be less than
    79→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    80→
    81→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    82→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
    83→
    84→Port used between sentry and bottle containers for entry traffic.
    85→Must be less than
    86→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    87→
    88→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    89→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}
    90→
    91→Base IPv4 address that defines the enclave network range.
    92→
    93→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    94→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
    95→
    96→Network mask width for the enclave network addressing.
    97→Valid range: 8-30.
    98→
    99→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   100→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
   101→
   102→IP address assigned to the sentry container for traffic mediation within the enclave.
   103→
   104→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   105→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   106→
   107→IP address assigned to the bottle container within the enclave.
   108→
   109→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   110→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}
   111→
   112→Minimum port number for bottle-initiated outbound connections.
   113→Must be greater than any entry port.
   114→
   115→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   116→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   117→
   118→Selects DNS resolution behavior for bottle-initiated requests.
   119→
   120→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   121→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   122→
   123→Selects IP access behavior for bottle-initiated connections.
   124→
   125→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   126→{rbrn_group_access_allowlist}
   127→
   128→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
   129→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   130→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}
   131→
   132→Variables in this group apply only when
   133→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   134→is
   135→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}.
   136→
   137→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   138→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   139→
   140→CIDR ranges for allowed bottle-initiated outbound traffic.
   141→

   398→// ============================================================================
   399→// TIER 1: WHY AND WHAT
   400→// ============================================================================
   401→
   402→== System Overview
   403→
   404→=== Core Trust Challenge
   405→
   406→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   407→{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   408→
   409→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   410→
   411→*
   412→{st_container_s}
   413→provide powerful tool isolation
   414→* But they need controlled access to do useful work
   415→* Standard
   416→{st_container}
   417→networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   418→
   419→=== The Bottle Pattern
   420→
   421→{at_rbm_system}
   422→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   423→{st_container}
   424→type here referred to as a
   425→{at_bottle_container}:
   426→
   427→. File Access Boundary
   428→*
   429→{at_bottle_container}
   430→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   431→{st_volume_mount_s}
   432→* No other access to
   433→{at_workstation}
   434→filesystem
   435→
   436→. Network Boundary
   437→*
   438→{at_bottle_container}
   439→has no direct network access
   440→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   441→{at_sentry_container},
   442→a
   443→{st_container}
   444→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   445→* Only declared
   446→{st_port_map_s}
   447→and destinations allowed
   448→* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic
   449→
   450→Each
   451→{at_bottle_service}
   452→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   453→
   454→* One
   455→{at_sentry_container}
   456→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   457→* One
   458→{at_censer_container}
   459→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   460→* One
   461→{at_bottle_container}
   462→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   463→* Clear configuration in a single
   464→{at_nameplate}
   465→file
   466→
   467→The
   468→{at_censer_container}
   469→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   470→{at_sentry_container},
   471→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   472→{at_bottle_container}
   473→via `--net=container:censer`.
   474→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   475→{at_bottle_container}
   476→experiences only a functional path to its
   477→{at_sentry_container}
   478→gateway
   479→
   480→=== Security Properties
   481→
   482→The
   483→{at_rbm_system}
   484→maintains following essential guarantees:
   485→
   486→. File Access Control
   487→*
   488→{at_bottle_container_s}
   489→can access only explicitly configured
   490→{st_volume_mount_s}
   491→* No access to broader system resources
   492→
   493→. Network Isolation
   494→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   495→{at_sentry_container}
   496→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   497→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   498→* Only explicitly configured
   499→{st_port_map_s}
   500→allow traffic between

2012→[[axvr_broach]]
  2013→{axvr_broach}::
  2014→Voices a regime broach operation definition.
  2015→The annotated term defines the silent initialization phase for a
  2016→{axrg_regime}:
  2017→setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
  2018→
  2019→[[axvr_validate]]
  2020→{axvr_validate}::
  2021→Voices a regime validate operation definition.
  2022→The annotated term defines the strict verification phase for a
  2023→{axrg_regime}:
  2024→checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
  2025→
  2026→[[axvr_render]]
  2027→{axvr_render}::
  2028→Voices a regime render operation definition.
  2029→The annotated term defines the diagnostic display phase for a
  2030→{axrg_regime}:
  2031→producing human-readable presentation of current state.
  2032→Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
  2033→
  2034→=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
  2035→
  2036→Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
  2037→Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
  2038→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2039→from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
  2040→
  2041→The surface syntax:
  2042→
  2043→[source,asciidoc]
  2044→----
  2045→//axhrb_regime
  2046→{rbrv_regime}
  2047→
  2048→//axhrgv_variable
  2049→{rbrv_sigil}
  2050→----
  2051→
  2052→The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
  2053→Following text contains the
  2054→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2055→that the marker reads ahead.
  2056→
  2057→==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
  2058→
  2059→[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
  2060→|===
  2061→|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
  2062→
  2063→|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
  2064→|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
  2065→|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
  2066→|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
  2067→|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
  2068→|{axhro_broach}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level broach operation for this regime
  2069→|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
  2070→|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
  2071→|===
  2072→
  2073→[[axhrb_regime]]
  2074→{axhrb_regime}::
  2075→Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
  2076→Lookahead: 1
  2077→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2078→— the regime this subdoc describes.
  2079→The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
  2080→{axvr_regime}
  2081→in the parent document.
